name: Zhinu
shortDescription: Zhinu, also known as the Weaver Girl, is a central figure in Chinese mythology, renowned for her weaving skills and her tragic love story with Niulang, the Cowherd.
description: Zhinu, the Weaver Girl, is a prominent figure in Chinese folklore and mythology, primarily known for her role in the Qixi Festival (Double Seventh Festival). She is often depicted as the seventh daughter of the Jade Emperor, tasked with weaving beautiful clouds and celestial garments. Her exceptional weaving skills were highly valued in the heavens. However, Zhinu yearned for a life beyond her weaving duties. One day, she descended to earth and encountered Niulang, a humble cowherd. They fell deeply in love and married, leading a happy life with their children. This transgression angered the Jade Emperor, who commanded Zhinu to return to the heavens and resume her weaving. The Queen Mother of the West (Xi Wangmu) created a celestial river (the Milky Way) to separate the lovers permanently. Moved by their devotion, the Jade Emperor allowed them to meet once a year on the seventh day of the seventh lunar month, when magpies form a bridge across the Milky Way. Zhinu symbolizes love, diligence, and the importance of family. Her story serves as a poignant reminder of the challenges faced by lovers and the enduring power of devotion. The Qixi Festival, celebrated in her honor, remains a significant cultural event in China, honoring love and relationships.
quests: Crossing the Milky Way on Qixi

sources.title: In Search of the Unicorn: The Mythology and Folklore of China
sources.author: Norman Girardot
